name: Vanuatu
headline: Vanuatu PRE-VOTE BASELINE CONFIRMATION at 35.9 / Developing — final pre-UNGA-vote position locked in 24 hours before the May 20 ICJ climate resolution vote. US pressure to withdraw refused; broad cross-regional coalition holding (Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Netherlands, Kenya, Singapore, Barbados, Jamaica, Philippines, Sierra Leone, Burkina Faso). Active climate-leadership INT signal sustained. No new dimensional weight beyond May 18 carry-forward. Post-vote reassessment scheduled for May 21.

baselineCorrection.note: Scanner flagged baseline mismatch RS=33.9 vs INDEX=35.9 (diff 2.0, RS_LOW). Per assessor protocol, INDEX is canonical truth. Applied 35.9.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. UNGA resolution sponsorship is pre-vote pending-action.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Domestic gender gap and gender-based violence remain counterweights to climate INT uplift.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. Co-sponsorship of a UNGA resolution operationalizing the ICJ Advisory Opinion is structural-climate-accountability work, but scoring weight pending vote outcome.

INT.rationale: Sustained. I1 consistency-under-pressure: Vanuatu's refusal to withdraw the resolution under documented US pressure (climatechangenews May 7) is a strong INT positive signal, already absorbed into May 18 carry-forward. Pre-vote position holds; post-vote upgrade window opens May 20-21.

evidence.finding: Vanuatu's UNGA resolution operationalizing the ICJ Advisory Opinion on climate change obligations — broad cross-regional co-sponsorship including Netherlands, Kenya, Sierra Leone, Singapore, Barbados, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Jamaica, Philippines, Burkina Faso.
US actively pressured Vanuatu to withdraw the resolution; Vanuatu refused. ICJ follow-up resolution framed as a test of UN climate leadership.
HRW formally urged governments to support Vanuatu's UN climate resolution.
UN human rights experts urged states to support the General Assembly resolution operationalising the ICJ Advisory Opinion.

keyEvidence: Baseline correction: 35.9 (INDEX canonical) applied; RS value 33.9 discarded per protocol.
UNGA vote scheduled May 20 — final pre-vote position locked.
US pressure to withdraw refused (INT-positive, carry-forward).
Cross-regional co-sponsorship coalition holding (12+ nations across 6 regions).
Post-vote reassessment scheduled May 21; ACC-positive scoring window opens if resolution passes.
rationale: Pre-vote baseline confirmation at 35.9. The May 19 evidence base is materially the same as May 18 — the vote is now within 24 hours. Lock the pre-vote baseline so post-vote movement (if resolution passes) can be cleanly attributed. Math: (2.5+2.5+2.5+2+2.5+2.5+2.5+2.5)/8 = 19.5/8 = 2.4375 → ((2.4375-1)/4)*100 = 35.94 → 35.9.
whyHeadline: Pre-UNGA-vote baseline locked; vote tomorrow tests Pacific climate-leadership coalition.

dominantDimension.note: INT carries the strongest carry-forward signal (US pressure refused) but no dimensional change applied pre-vote.

nextForwardSignal.label: UNGA vote on Vanuatu ICJ climate resolution

watches: Co-sponsor cluster behavior (Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Sierra Leone, Kenya)
Post-vote ACC and SYS upgrade window
boundaryWatchResolution: Not boundary-sensitive. 35.9 sits 5.1pt below Functional floor (40/41), 15.9pt above Critical/Developing floor.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
